summary: Matched the 136-byte stage function `grKongo_801D828C` in `src/melee/gr/grkongo.c` by adding a small implementation that reads Kongo-specific ground variables, asserts a retained `taru.keep` pointer, checks byte 2 of that pointed data for value 8, then clears state and calls `grMaterial_801C95C4`. The PR also introduced `struct grKongo_GroundVars` in `src/melee/gr/types.h` and added it to the `Ground::gv` union, enabling typed access to offsets around `gp+C4` through `gp+D0`. Scratch config entries for nearby labels/functions at `0x801D0B0C`, `0x801D4EEC`, and `0x801D828C` were promoted to `OK`.
what_changed: Implemented `void grKongo_801D828C(HSD_GObj* gobj)` using `Ground* gp = gobj->user_data`.
Added `HSD_ASSERT(1719, gp->gv.kongo.u.taru.keep)` before dereferencing the retained taru-related pointer.
The function checks `((u8*) gp->gv.kongo.u.taru.keep)[2] == 8`; when true, it clears `xC6`, nulls `taru.keep`, and calls `grMaterial_801C95C4(gobj)`.
Added `struct grKongo_GroundVars` with fields at documented offsets `gp+C4`, `gp+C6`, `gp+C8`, `gp+CC`, and a union at `gp+D0` containing `taru.keep`.
Added `struct grKongo_GroundVars kongo;` to the `Ground::gv` union.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` to mark `func_801D828C` as `OK`; the same hunk also changed `lbl_801D0B0C` and `lbl_801D4EEC` to `OK`.
smart_moves: Introduced a minimal stage-specific ground-vars struct instead of accessing raw offsets directly in the matched C function.
Used a nested union/struct shape `gp->gv.kongo.u.taru.keep`, preserving room for future naming and variant-specific Kongo ground state while matching the current access pattern.
Kept uncertain fields as `UNK_T` and named only the behavior-supported member `taru.keep`, avoiding overclaiming unknown structure semantics.
Used a byte cast for `taru.keep` before indexing `[2]`, which directly models the observed byte-level condition without inventing a pointed-to struct.
Preserved the original assertion line number with `HSD_ASSERT(1719, ...)`, an important matching detail for generated code and rodata/assert behavior.
Updated scratch status to `OK` after the function match so matching progress tracking reflects the decompiled state.
decomp_lessons: For ground-stage functions, matching often depends on adding the correct stage-specific member to the shared `Ground::gv` union so accesses become typed and offset-stable.
When the pointed-to object type is unknown, a cast to `u8*` plus a byte offset can be preferable to creating a speculative struct.
Small control-flow details matter: early return on `xC6 != 1`, assert before dereference, then conditional state reset and material call are all part of the match shape.
Offset comments in header structs, such as `/* gp+C6 */`, are useful breadcrumbs for future decomp work that needs to align `Ground` substructures with observed assembly offsets.
A function match may require both source implementation and header modeling; matching code in `grkongo.c` depended on extending `types.h`.
naming_conventions: `grKongo_801D828C` follows the project convention of stage/module prefix plus address when a semantic name is not yet established.
Stage-specific ground variable structs use names like `grKongo_GroundVars`, alongside existing `GroundVars_izumi`, `GroundVars_izumi2`, and `GroundVars_izumi3`.
Unknown fields retain offset-style names such as `xC4`, `xC6`, `xC8`, and `xCC`.
The added nested member name `taru.keep` suggests a barrel-related Kongo state field, but the evidence only establishes the member path used for matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Modeled a halfword/short state field at `gp+C6` as `short xC6`, matching comparisons and assignment to values `1` and `0`.
Used `UNK_T` for pointer-like or unknown-width fields at `gp+C8`, `gp+CC`, and `gp+D0` rather than forcing precise types prematurely.
Used `((u8*) keep)[2] == 8` to express a byte load from offset 2 of an unknown object.
Included `HSD_ASSERT` with the exact observed line number `1719`, which can affect matching through assert metadata.
Called `grMaterial_801C95C4(gobj)` only after clearing the Kongo state fields, preserving side-effect order.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that rename or refine `grKongo_GroundVars` fields `xC4`, `xC6`, `xC8`, `xCC`, or `taru.keep`.
Look for later matches of other Kongo functions that clarify the type of the object pointed to by `gp->gv.kongo.u.taru.keep`.
Check whether `lbl_801D0B0C` and `lbl_801D4EEC` were already matched elsewhere or were incidentally marked `OK` by this scratch update.
Search for other stage functions calling `grMaterial_801C95C4` after clearing ground-variable state.
